meaning: n.(东欧或西伯利亚的) 坟头,坟墩
examples: Kurgan tribe known as the Yusak, savage and ferocious warriors as much at home on horseback as on foot, had heard of Grimgor's approach and gathered to meet him.
Once or twice under the foothills I glimpsed the mound of a kurgan, broken open like the lips of a volcano – the burial-place of a tribal chief, perhaps, or the milestone of some lost nomad advance.

Eventually she found what she was looking for in a computer record at Mamayev Kurgan - just two miles (3km) from her home.
His body was found in 1961 and reburied in Mamayev Kurgan.
